content:
📜 4. Some Rituals Require Group Participation

📜 Certain rituals (fate manipulation, purges, legendary item activations) now require a collective decision.

✔ How Group Rituals Work:
• Some rituals require the entire class to vote on whether they succeed.
• Some rituals require a certain number of participants (ex: three players minimum).
• Some rituals may be sabotaged by a hidden countermeasure (ex: an unknown player works against the ritual).

✔ Examples of Group Rituals:
• The Trial of Ascension → Requires majority vote to allow a player to transform into a unique archetype.
• The Fate Rewriting Ceremony → Requires at least three players to activate a fate reversal.
• The Summoning of a Lost Relic → Requires a faction-wide agreement to bring back a vanished item.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Encourages strategic player alliances and betrayals.
✔ Prevents rituals from being isolated actions—forces the class to engage.
✔ Makes group-based play more dynamic and unpredictable.
